foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Acid-base buffering systems (bicarbonate, phosphate, protein) are core homeostatic mechanisms; surgical injury causes metabolic acidosis that triggers compensatory respiratory and renal responses

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: The adrenal medullary catecholamine response is the primary neuroendocrine mediator of the acute stress response to surgical injury, driving tachycardia, vasoconstriction, and glycogenolysis

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Shock represents decompensated homeostatic failure; understanding normal compensatory mechanisms (baroreceptor reflexes, RAAS, ADH) is essential to comprehend shock pathophysiology

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Major burns trigger the most extreme metabolic response to injury with hypermetabolism, catecholamine surge, and massive fluid shifts disrupting homeostasis

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Fluid and electrolyte balance is the most clinically relevant homeostatic mechanism in surgery; Na/K/Ca regulation underpins the metabolic response to surgical injury

objectives: SU1.1: Describe basic concepts of homeostasis, enumerate the metabolic
SU1.2: Describe the factors that affect the metabolic response to injury.
SU1.3: Describe basic concepts of perioperative care.
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Homeostasis across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
